questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "The Eiffel Tower was designed ___"
questions.answer: by the engineer Gustave Eiffel.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "The Great Wall of China was built ___"
questions.answer: by thousands of workers.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "The famous painting was stolen ___"
questions.answer: and was never found.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "The new hospital was opened ___"
questions.answer: by the president last year.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Two windows were broken in the storm ___"
questions.answer: but nobody was hurt.

questions.prompt: The car _____ (repair) by a mechanic yesterday.
questions.answer: was repaired — 'The car was repaired' — singular subject, so use 'was' + past participle of 'repair'.
were sold — 'All the tickets were sold' — plural subject, so use 'were' + past participle of 'sell'.
wasn't sent — 'The letter wasn't sent' — negative: 'was not' + past participle.
Was / finished — 'Was the report finished?' — question form: 'Was' + subject + past participle.

questions.prompt: Three students _____ (choose) to represent the school.
questions.answer: were chosen — 'Three students were chosen' — plural subject, so use 'were' + past participle of 'choose'.

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "The homework was gave back by the teacher."
questions.answer: Use the past participle: 'was given back', not 'was gave back'.

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "The windows were broke during the storm."
questions.answer: The past participle of 'break' is 'broken': 'were broken'.

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "The emails was sent early in the morning."
questions.answer: 'Emails' is plural, so use 'were': 'The emails were sent'.

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "The trophy was win by a young student."
questions.answer: Use the past participle: 'won', not 'win': 'was won by'.

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "Was the package delivered by the postman yesterday?"
questions.answer: Actually this sentence is correct — 'by the postman' is the agent and 'delivered' is the right past participle. Wait — let me rephrase: Were the packages delivered by the postman yesterday is correct; the original has a different issue. The mistake is none — see replacement below.
The bridge was built by local engineers.
The match wasn't broadcast on TV.
Was the painting stolen from the museum?
All the invitations were sent last week.
The new school was opened by the president.
